prompt: A developer finishes configuration changes on a sub-production instance and needs to bundle them for movement to test. Which mechanism captures the changes?
choices: An Update Set that records modified application files
An Import Set that stages CSV rows only
An ACL that grants write on sys_update_set
A homepage gauge showing pending changes
explanation: Update Sets group configuration changes so they can be completed, exported, retrieved, previewed, and committed on another instance.

prompt: A team completes an update set in development, retrieves it in test, previews the changes, and commits it in test. Which sequence reflects correct update set promotion?
choices: Complete in dev, retrieve in test, preview in test, commit in test
Commit in dev, delete in test, retrieve in prod, preview in dev
Preview in dev, retrieve in dev, complete in test, commit in prod only
Retrieve in dev, complete in test, commit without preview in prod
explanation: Update sets are completed on the source instance, retrieved on the target, previewed to review impact, then committed on the target.

prompt: How should a scoped application be moved to another instance when instances do not share an update source?
choices: Publish to an application repository or Store and install on the target instance
Retype every table and script manually on the target
Email configuration screenshots to the target admin
Delete target instance tables before import
explanation: Scoped apps are distributed by publishing to a repository or Store for installation, or via source control apply-remote on the target.

prompt: Which source control operations are available for a scoped app linked to Git in Studio? (Choose two.)
choices: Commit local changes to a branch
Apply remote repository changes into the instance
Rename the sys_user table globally
Disable the instance authentication realm
explanation: Git integration supports committing instance changes to a branch and applying remote changes back into the application on the instance.

prompt: Before deploying a downloaded Store application into production, what practice reduces risk?
choices: Install and validate the application in a sub-production instance first
Disable every ACL on production before install
Convert the application to global scope on production
Delete all existing scoped apps on production
explanation: Applications should be installed and tested in sub-production before production promotion to avoid disrupting live services.
